data.title: Airbus names new head of helicopters unit
data.description:
European planemaker Airbus (EADSF) (EADSY) said it named Matthieu Louvot as the next chief executive of its helicopters division, effective in April.

Louvot will succeed Bruno Even, who has led the business since 2018 and is stepping down for personal reasons, the company said.

data.title: Dutch Bros adds Clutch Coffee as its first notable M&A deal
data.description:
Dutch Bros (BROS) struck a deal this week to acquire Clutch Coffee Bar.

Clutch Coffee Bar is a regional drive-thru coffee brand founded in 2018 in Mooresville, North Carolina, by Darren Spicer and two co-founders, with the goal of building a high-energy, customer-centric drive-thru beverage concept. The brand grew across the Carolinas by emphasizing fast, personable service, a lively atmosphere, and a menu built around coffee, energy drinks, and other specialty beverages. The sale announcement was made via an open letter from Darren Spicer, the founder and CEO of Clutch Coffee Bar.

Spicer highlighted that the acquisition would provide Clutch team members with growth opportunities within the Dutch Bros (BROS) system.

Dutch Bros (BROS) plans a short closure and renovation period for 20 Clutch stores that will be rebranded as Dutch Bros shops. "We're excited to continue expanding across the country as we work toward our long-term goal of 2,029 shops in 2029," read a statement from Dutch Bros. The acquisition could incrementally strengthen the company's position in the fast-growing U.S. drive-thru coffee segment.

Shares of Dutch Bros (BROS) inched up 0.2% in premarket trading on Wednesday. The coffee chain stock has traded in a narrow range ($60-$63) for the early part of 2026.

data.title: Chinese chip designer Montage secures backing from Alibaba ahead of HK listing: report
data.description:
The Shanghai-based Montage Technology, which produces chips to increase the speed of data flows in data centers and artificial intelligence accelerators, has secured support from Alibaba (BABA) and JPMorgan Asset Management (JPM) as it prepares for listing on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, according to Bloomberg.

Alibaba was up 2.3% during early trading on Wednesday.

The listing, which might occur before the end of this month, is expected to raise about $900M, the report said. Other cornerstone investors in the deal include Aberdeen Group, Mirae Asset Securities and UBS Group. This would be Montage's second listing, as it is already traded in Shanghai.

Montage's 2024 revenue totaled $521.27M with a net profit of $196M and a gross margin of 58%. Net profit was expected to reach $329.6M in 2025 and then $427.9M in 2026, according to the company's prospectus.

In November, Montage was named to Forbes China's "2025 Top Most Innovative Companies" list. It was also named to this list in 2023.

"As the inventor of the DDR4 '1+9' distributed buffer memory subsystem architecture and a pioneer in DDR5 technology, the company serves on the board of JEDEC, where it has spearheaded international standards development for DDR5 RCD, MDB, and CKD chips," Montage said. 

Earlier this month, Alibaba also backed MiniMax, which develops multimodal AI models, in its Hong Kong listing.

data.title: AppLovin in focus as Evercore starts with Outperform rating
data.description:
AppLovin (APP) was in focus on Wednesday as Evercore ISI started coverage on the mobile software company with an Outperform rating and a $835 price target.

Shares were fractionally higher in premarket trading.

“APP is the dominant ad tech platform for mobile gaming with an emerging e-commerce performance channel, which we view as a material [total addressable market] expander,” analyst Robert Coolbirth wrote in a note to clients. “Combined, we expect mobile gaming and e-commerce ad spend to sustain 30%+ revenue and EBITDA CAGRs from ’25 to ’28, and believe that APP can maintain its current 36x FY26 EV/EBITDA valuation multiple.”

Delving deeper, Coolbirth said there are two key debates: how long the runway is for the company's core mobile gaming business; and how large AppLovin's e-commerce advertising business can be.

On the mobile gaming side, Coolbirth said there is “ample” room to grow, “given solid underlying industry dynamics, catalysts, and APP’s superior execution.” For the e-commerce business, it's “too soon to tell” if AppLovin can compete with Google (GOOG) (GOOGL), Meta (META) and Amazon (AMZN), but industry checks and third-party pixel tracking suggest strong momentum.

“We believe APP can achieve [high-single-digit to low-double-digit] penetration of DTC e-commerce advertiser spend in FY28 (i.e., $3.4B of revenue on $7.5B of ad spend vs. a $75B-$150B ads TAM, supported by ~$750B+ of global DTC GMV),” Coolbirth wrote.

Other areas of debate include deplatforming risk, which Coolbirth said is “truly remote,” though Apple (AAPL) could be more aggressive in its conversion tracking practices.

Upcoming potential catalysts include any moves that mobile game publishers make to boost user acquisition, as monetization mechanics improve; momentum in e-commerce; the company's “strong” operating model, with Coolbirth forecasting an adjusted EBITDA margin of 82.5% for fiscal 2025; and the company's upcoming fourth-quarter earnings report, set to be released on Feb. 11. 

“We think Q4 results will likely showcase strong e-commerce momentum and continued gaming strength,” Coolbirth explained. “With the discontinuation of the Array carrier downloads business in October, we think a strong Q4 can also help refute a pillar of the bear case.”

data.title: HUB Cyber Security announces 1-for-15 reverse share split; shares down
data.description:
• HUB Cyber Security (HUBC) will implement a 1-for-15 reverse share split of its ordinary shares effective on January 15, 2026.
  • The reverse share split is intended to increase the share price and help the company maintain compliance with Nasdaq’s minimum bid price requirement.
  • HUBC shares down 34.5% premarket on Wednesday.

data.title: IonQ appoints Katie Arrington as chief information officer
data.description:
• IonQ (IONQ) announced on Wednesday that national security leader and technology advocate Katie Arrington will join its executive team as chief information officer (CIO), reporting to IonQ chief operating officer and chief financial officer Inder Singh, beginning January 19, 2026.
  • As CIO, Arrington will oversee the protection and modernization of IonQ’s global enterprise systems, safeguard digital assets and supply chains, and strengthen operational and cyber resilience across the company’s rapidly expanding footprint.
  • Most recently, Arrington performed the duties of the Department of War’s Chief Information Officer, advising the Secretary on enterprise information management; satellite, communications, and spectrum capabilities; cyber assurance; and space and emerging technology policy. Earlier, as Deputy CIO for Cybersecurity, she led defense-wide oversight of cyber strategy, governance, and compliance.
  • IonQ also announced that Leslie Kershaw, currently with IonQ Capella, will advance into a newly expanded role as chief information security officer (CISO), reporting to Arrington.
  • IONQ -0.49% premarket to $48.7.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Wednesday: BAC, WFC, HON, XPEV
data.description:
Stock index futures fell on Wednesday as the wholesale inflation report came in as expected. Here are the four stocks to watch on the day:

XPeng (XPEV) slipped 0.33% in premarket trading following reports that the Chinese electric vehicle maker has set a target of selling 550,000 to 600,000 vehicles in 2026. Sources indicate that the target was revealed at an internal strategy meeting at the Guangzhou-based company, according to Chinese media site 36Kr.

Bank of America (BAC) fell 0.44% before the opening bell after the bank turned in Q4 earnings and revenue that topped Wall Street expectations. Both net interest income and noninterest income climbed past consensus estimates, reflecting a resilient consumer and businesses.

Honeywell (HON) gained 2.11% in premarket trade after the company confirmed that it intends to submit an S-1 filing with the SEC for its quantum computing unit, Quantinuum, and have it go public. The Charlotte, N.C.-based conglomerate did not mention a valuation for Quantinuum, but the unit raised $600 million in 2025 at a $10 billion valuation.

Wells Fargo (WFC) slipped 1.9% in premarket trading after the company turned in mixed Q4 results and 2026 guidance. Q4 net interest income was lower than Wall Street expected, leading to a miss on the top line.

data.title: The RMR Group earns $23.6 million of incentive business management fees in 2025
data.description:
• The RMR Group (RMR) said on Wednesday that a total of $23.6 million of incentive business management fees have been earned for 2025, based on the three-year measurement period ended December 31, 2025. 
  • These incentive business management fees will be paid to RMR in January.
  • RMR +0.24% premarket to $16.42.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Obamacare enrollments drop nearly 1M amid premium hikes
data.description:
As of the first week of January, nearly 22.8M Americans have signed up for Affordable Care Act (ACA) plans for 2026, roughly 1M less than the same period last year, amid rising plan premiums following the expiry of COVID-era ACA tax credits.

According to a national snapshot released by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services ((CMS)), about 22.8 million consumers have signed up for ACA plans, also known as Obamacare, as of Jan. 3, since the open enrollment period started on Nov. 1, 2025.

More than 2.8M, or roughly 12%, of total signups were new to the ACA exchanges, and the rest already had 2025 coverage and chose exchanges to select a new plan or were automatically added for 2026 coverage.

Last year, CMS’s national snapshot indicated that about 23.6M Americans had selected health insurance through Obamacare exchanges for the 2025 plan year as of Jan. 4. When the OEP for HealthCare.gov exchanges lapsed on Jan. 15, the coverage had expanded to roughly 24.2M.

The nonpartisan Congressional Budget Office had previously projected a 2.2M decline in Obamacare enrollees this year after the Trump administration moved to end the enhanced premium tax credits granted in 2021 for those who buy insurance through the ACA exchanges.

With the U.S. Congress unable to find an alternative to expiring tax credits since then, more than 22M Americans buying health insurance through ACA exchanges faced an 114% rise in their monthly health premiums from Jan. 1, according to healthcare policy research organization KFF.

data.title: Oracle seen as 'undervalued' after recent sell-off, KeyBanc says
data.description:
Following the sell-off that lopped off nearly a third of its market cap since late September, shares of Oracle (ORCL) are “undervalued,” KeyBanc Capital Markets said.

Oracle shares fell fractionally in premarket trading on Wednesday.

“Valuation is always subjective, but a pitch for under- or over-valuation based on a sum-of-the-parts methodology has an extra whiff of desperation,” KeyBanc analyst Jackson Ader wrote in a note to clients. “There is a little bit of scolding the market and a lot a bit of yelling into the void. We know this and are doing it anyway—twice. We separate Oracle into its core (the applications and database Oracle we know from the early part of the decade) and the infrastructure business (IaaS/OCI). Spoiler alert: our estimates indicate either one or both are being undervalued, implying Oracle's stock overall remains attractive.”

Ader, who has an Overweight rating and $300 price target on Oracle, said the company's core business (applications and database), is worth roughly $125 per share, based on peer earnings multiples. Assuming $125 per share for the core business, its infrastructure-as-a-service business (which includes Oracle Cloud Infrastructure) is “undervalued,” trading at 3.5 times revenue, or worth roughly between $75 and $80 per share, well below where neocloud companies are trading. If the IaaS business were to be given a multiple similar to the neoclouds, that would imply it's worth around $135 per share, Ader explained.

Neocloud companies are often defined as CoreWeave (CRWV) and Nebius (NBIS), but Ader did not explicitly mention them by name.

“Based on the trajectory of the business of late and going forward, we assumed a heavy weight of the estimated operating expenses to be placed on the faster-growing IaaS business,” Ader continued. “Coupling this with the fact that Oracle was generating high 30s% GAAP EBIT margins in FY21, prior to the OCI investments, we believe the core business can be run at GAAP EBIT margins of 41-42% by CY28. We also assume that the vast majority of the Company's overall interest expense should be allocated to IaaS in getting to the subdivision of our EPS estimates.”
